senses.definition: the process of restoring someone’s human qualities or dignity, or making something more human again
examples: The policy aims at the rehumanization of refugees through better integration programs.
Proponents argue that rehumanization is essential in debates about controversial technologies.

senses.definition: to restore someone’s human qualities or dignity; to make something more humane
examples: The charity seeks to rehumanize migrants by showing their humanity.
Policies should rehumanize discourse around mental health.

senses.definition: past participle of rehumanize; having been made more humane or given back human qualities
examples: The program left many refugees rehumanized after years of neglect.
Arehumanized approach to policy emphasized personal stories.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of rehumanize; makes someone or something more human
examples: The narrative rehumanizes the characters, making readers care about them.
New policy proposals rehumanizes the discussion around asylum.

senses.definition: the act of making something more humane or restoring human qualities
examples: The film focuses on the rehumanizing of previously voiceless groups.
An ongoing project is rehumanizing the way we talk about refugees.

senses.definition: to humble again; to reduce someone’s pride or status again
examples: The mistake could rehumble the once-confident leader.
The new evidence rehumbles the previous claims.

senses.definition: to humiliate someone again
examples: The press attempted to rehumiiliate the public figure.
He refused to rehumiiliate the rival, despite the dispute.

senses.definition: past participle of rehumiliate; having been humiliated again
examples: The speaker felt rehumiiliated after the controversy.
She endured rehumiiliated remarks during the debate.

senses.definition: the act of humiliating someone again
examples: The crowd engaged in rehumiiliating chants.
Rehumiliating remarks were not allowed in the meeting.

senses.definition: the act or site of humiliating again; a renewed humiliation
examples: The report details the rehumiiliation of the athlete.
Public rehumiiliation can have lasting effects.

senses.definition: a part of a plant stem that must be cut or a term in certain dialects; also a possible typographical artifact meaning to rehang or to perform a musical rebuff in some contexts
examples: In botany, the term rehung is rarely used today.
The carpenter noted the rehung of the frame was needed.

senses.definition: to combine again to form new hybrid structures, often in biology or chemistry
examples: Researchers plan to rehybridize the gene to study its effects.
The team attempted to rehybridize crops for resilience.

senses.definition: able to be rehydrated; capable of absorbing water again
examples: The powder is rehydratable to form a solution.
Use this tablet while it is still rehydratable.

senses.definition: to restore water content to something; to become hydrated again
examples: Add water to rehydrate the dried fruit.
The body needs fluids to rehydrate after exercise.

senses.definition: having had water added back to something or regained hydration
examples: The rehydrated fruit was plump again.
Rehydrated noodles are ready to serve.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of rehydrate; adds water to something
examples: The mix rehydrates quickly when stirred with hot water.
These tablets rehydrate the powder into a drink.

senses.definition: the process of adding water again to something; becoming hydrated again
examples: Rehydrating the dried soup is simple with hot water.
Rehydrating medications can improve absorption.

senses.definition: the act of adding water to something or the state of being hydrated again
examples: Oral rehydration is important after a bout of illness.
Severe dehydration requires medical rehydration.

senses.definition: plural of rehydration; instances of restoring water content
examples: The clinic recorded several rehydrations for patients with diarrhea.
Different rehydrations may be recommended for athletes.

senses.definition: to induce hypnosis again; to place back under hypnosis
examples: The therapist attempted to rehypnotise the patient to reinforce the session.
Practitioners must be careful not to rehypnotise participants without consent.

senses.definition: past tense of rehypnotise; was subjected to hypnosis again
examples: The subject was rehypnotised to uncover more details.
She was rehypnotised with consent.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of rehypnotise; hypnotizes again
examples: The clinician rehypnotises the patient when needed.
If the therapy fails, the clinician rehypnotises the subject under supervision.

senses.definition: the act of hypnotizing again; performing hypnosis anew
examples: The therapist focused on rehypnotising the subject for deeper recall.
Rehypnotising was used with caution in the experiment.

senses.definition: to hypnotize again; to induce hypnotic state once more
examples: Researchers planned to rehypnotize participants to test memory effects.
Use caution when you rehypnotize someone.

senses.definition: past tense of rehypnotize; hypnotized again
examples: The subject was rehypnotized to retrieve the memory.
He was rehypnotized under careful supervision.

senses.definition: describing someone who has been hypnotized again
examples: The subject appeared rehypnotized after the session.
A rehypnotized state can last for minutes.

senses.definition: the act of hypnotizing again; performing hypnosis anew
examples: Rehypnotising can produce new memory cues.
The staff discussed rehypnotising under strict protocol.

senses.definition: to place under hypnosis again
examples: Clinicians may rehypnotize to explore hidden memories.
The team planned to rehypnotize the volunteer with consent.

senses.definition: past tense of rehypnotize; hypnotized again
examples: The participant was rehypnotized to verify the memory.
She was rehypnotized later in the study.
